title: Check Station Switch Input States On VISU_IO_DIAG Screen
summary: Use the read-only VISU_IO_DIAG screen on the Operator Station HMI to observe which station-related inputs are shown as on or off and, when needed, select an input section to open a window with more information about the inputs.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when troubleshooting from the Operator Station HMI and you need to inspect the station-mounted switch input states or view additional information about displayed inputs on the VISU_IO_DIAG screen.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to perform corrective actions, because the source only documents observation and information viewing on this screen.
Do not use this procedure to infer undocumented meanings for on/off states beyond what the screen displays.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the VISU_IO_DIAG screen, observe displayed input states, open available input detail windows, and record the displayed information.

safety_notes: VISU_IO_DIAG is a read-only screen and can be used for troubleshooting.
Do not infer undocumented meanings for on/off states beyond what the screen displays.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Operator Station HMI

access_required: Access to the Operator Station HMI
system_preconditions: VISU_IO_DIAG screen is available on the Operator Station HMI

visual_references.description: Operator Station VISU_IO_DIAG read-only diagnostics screen showing station switch input area and selectable input sections.

steps.title: Open the VISU_IO_DIAG screen
steps.purpose: Access the read-only diagnostics screen used for troubleshooting and input observation.
steps.instruction: Open the VISU_IO_DIAG screen on the Operator Station HMI.
steps.expected_result: The VISU_IO_DIAG screen is displayed and available for read-only troubleshooting use.
steps.healthy_condition: The Operator Station HMI shows the VISU_IO_DIAG screen.
steps.failure_condition: The VISU_IO_DIAG screen cannot be opened or is not available on the Operator Station HMI.

actions.label: Open VISU_IO_DIAG
actions.exact_action: Open the VISU_IO_DIAG screen on the Operator Station HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Read-only HMI diagnostics screen for viewing operator station I/O status, alarms, temperatures, and voltage levels.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the VISU_IO_DIAG screen cannot be accessed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: VISU_IO_DIAG is an Operator Station HMI IO Diagnostics screen. The manual states it is read-only and can be used for troubleshooting.
NOTE: This screen is read-only and can be used for troubleshooting.

steps.title: Locate the station switch input section
steps.purpose: Find the screen area that corresponds to the switch mounted on the station.
steps.instruction: Locate the section on VISU_IO_DIAG that corresponds to the switch mounted on the station.
steps.expected_result: The station switch section is identified on the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The section corresponding to the station-mounted switch is visible and identifiable.
steps.failure_condition: The station switch section cannot be identified on the displayed screen.

actions.label: Find station switch section
actions.exact_action: Locate the section that corresponds to the switch mounted on the station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_IO_DIAG screen area corresponding to the station-mounted switch inputs.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the station switch section cannot be identified on VISU_IO_DIAG.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 4: This section corresponds to the switch mounted on the station and convey which inputs are on and off.
On VISU_IO_DIAG, one section corresponds to the switch mounted on the station and indicates which inputs are on or off.

steps.title: Observe displayed input on/off states
steps.purpose: Determine which station-related inputs are currently shown as on or off.
steps.instruction: Observe which inputs in the station switch section are shown as on and which are shown as off.
steps.expected_result: The displayed on/off state of the station-related inputs is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: Input states are visibly displayed as on or off on the screen.
steps.failure_condition: Input states are not visible, not readable, or cannot be determined from the screen.

actions.label: Observe input states
actions.exact_action: Observe which inputs are shown as on and which are shown as off.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The station switch section that conveys which inputs are on and off.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the displayed input states cannot be read from the screen.
Stop if interpretation would require assumptions not documented by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 4: This section corresponds to the switch mounted on the station and convey which inputs are on and off.

steps.title: Locate the input sections
steps.purpose: Identify the selectable input sections that can provide more detailed input information.
steps.instruction: Locate the input sections shown on the VISU_IO_DIAG screen.
steps.expected_result: The input sections are identified and available for selection.
steps.healthy_condition: Input sections are visible on the screen.
steps.failure_condition: Input sections are not visible or cannot be identified.

actions.label: Find input sections
actions.exact_action: Locate the input sections shown on the screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Read-only HMI diagnostics screen area showing the selectable input sections.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the input sections described by the source are not present.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 5: This section shows the inputs. Selecting one of these sections will open a window providing more information on what the inputs are.

steps.title: Open an input information window
steps.purpose: View additional source-backed information about a displayed input.
steps.instruction: Select one of the input sections to open the window that provides more information on what the inputs are.
steps.expected_result: A window opens with more information about the selected inputs.
steps.healthy_condition: Selecting an input section opens the additional information window.
steps.failure_condition: Selecting an input section does not open the additional information window.

actions.label: Select input section
actions.exact_action: Select one of the input sections to open the window that provides more information on what the inputs are.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Selectable input sections on VISU_IO_DIAG that can be used to open more detailed input information.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the input sections do not open the additional information window as described.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Item 5: This section shows the inputs. Selecting one of these sections will open a window providing more information on what the inputs are.
Another section shows inputs, and selecting it opens a window with more information about the inputs.

steps.title: Record displayed states and input details
steps.purpose: Capture the observed screen information for troubleshooting or follow-up review.
steps.instruction: Record the observed on/off states and any additional input information displayed.
steps.expected_result: The displayed input states and any additional input details are documented.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed on/off states and any opened input information are recorded without adding unsupported interpretation.
steps.failure_condition: Observed states or displayed input details are not recorded, or undocumented interpretations are added.

actions.label: Document observations
actions.exact_action: Record the observed on/off states and any additional input information displayed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if documenting the result would require inferring meanings not shown by the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The user can identify displayed station switch input states and retrieve additional source-backed input information from the VISU_IO_DIAG screen.
The source does not provide corrective actions or state interpretations, so none were added.

success_criteria: The VISU_IO_DIAG screen is opened on the Operator Station HMI.
The section corresponding to the station-mounted switch is identified.
Displayed station input states are observed as on or off.
At least one input section can be selected to open additional input information when needed.
Observed states and any displayed input details are recorded.
healthy_conditions: VISU_IO_DIAG is accessible and visible on the Operator Station HMI.
The station switch section clearly shows which inputs are on and off.
Selecting an input section opens a window with more information about the inputs.
failure_conditions: The station switch section or input sections cannot be identified.
Displayed input states cannot be read.
Selecting an input section does not open the additional information window.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the input sections do not open the additional information window as described.
Escalate if the VISU_IO_DIAG screen or required sections cannot be accessed or identified.
Do not infer undocumented meanings for on/off states beyond what the screen displays.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall VISU_IO_DIAG screen used for read-only troubleshooting, including station switch input area and selectable input sections.

Item 5: This section shows the inputs. Selecting one of these sections will open a window providing more information on what the inputs are.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide navigation steps for reaching VISU_IO_DIAG from another screen.
The source does not define the meaning of specific on/off input states.
The source does not provide corrective actions based on observed input states.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required.
